# LIMO

> LIMO (Local Intelligent Media Organizer) is a free, open-source, offline-first photo organizer for Windows that automatically categorizes photos and finds people by face search, entirely on-device, with no cloud upload or account required.

## About

LIMO is inspired by Google Picasa, the free desktop photo manager Google discontinued in 2016. It revives that simplicity with local computer vision: face recognition, automatic categorization (portraits, couples, groups, landscapes, documents), and a feedback loop (thumbs up/down) that improves face-search accuracy over time.

A common real-world use case: photo studios use LIMO's face search to locate a customer's past photo orders from a new snapshot or a scanned print, even when the customer has lost their order token or receipt number. The studio simply searches by face instead of by file name or order number.

## Key facts

- Free and open source, MIT License
- Windows desktop application, built with Python and PyQt6
- 100% offline: no cloud processing, no account, no telemetry
- Local face search comparing face embeddings entirely on-device
- Automatic photo categorization: portraits, couples, groups, landscapes, documents
- Peak memory usage kept under 500MB even on large photo libraries
- Current version: v1.0.0
